goalsHistory.active: Off-page; not directly visible in chapter1.
goalsHistory.underlying: N/A in on-page canon for this chapter.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Off-page; Jaina's action has moved beyond his direct supervision.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Still off-page in prose, though his institutional authority matters.

knowledge.detail: Referenced as the master whose apprentice would carry enough political and strategic weight to matter immediately inside Quel'Thalas.
Canon grounding: leader of the Kirin Tor and Jaina Proudmoore's mentor, making his name a meaningful credential and threat multiplier in border dossiers.
Chapter2 makes explicit that Jaina left him only a note; his authority is present more as missing sanction than as active support.
Because Jaina is acting without approval, Antonidas becomes the absent measure of legitimacy against which Sylvanas tests her story.

Chapter6 clarifies his intellectual shadow over the plague case: he is the one who closed certain Kirin Tor books and taught Jaina that some necromantic questions were safer left sealed than answered.

arc.currentPosition: Still an off-page authority, but chapter6 reframes him as more than missing sanction. He is also the ethical line behind Jaina's revulsion: the master who knew which doors should stay shut.
